EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Broad surface Trough over the Eastern Caribbean, combined with high moisture at low level of the atmosphere to produced numerous showers with thunderstorms over Saint Croix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Melvin Evans Highway near east Airport road was reported flooded. Isolated areas of the North Side road were also flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong tropical wave located few hundred miles east of the lesser Antilles became better organized on July 8, 2013 and was declared a Tropical Depression. Later, The National Hurricane Center named it as Tropical Storm Chantal. Chantal move generally west-northwest across the Caribbean Sea, passing arounf 150 miles south of Puerto Rico July 9, 2013. The outer bands associated with the circulation of Chantal, produced numerous showers with thunderstorms with strong gusty winds. Observed precipitation across the U. S. Virgin Islands showed that most rainfall was below an inch. No flooding was reported across the islands and official river gages did not approach flood stage at any time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A downed tree was reported on major Highway across mid island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Topical Storm Gabrille pass south of the local islands from September 5th and dissipated on September 6th southwest of the local area. Rainbands associated with the storm produced torrential rainfall mainly over Saint Thomas with several places flooded.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms associated with Tropical Storm Gabrielle produced heavy rainfall which led to urban flooding on Saint Croix. Northside road was flooded near the vicinity of Estate Profit and Diageo. Also Eastbound lane was completed flooded and obstructed. Several trees were down near the Sweeney Police Station and on south shore road south of Junies Bar.
